title: Robinhood's Crypto Revenue Surpasses Options in Q4 2024
text: Robinhood reported exceptional financial results for Q4 2024, with cryptocurrency emerging as its dominant revenue driver for the first time. The trading platform's total revenue reached $1.01 billion, significantly exceeding analyst expectations of $849.06 million. Crypto's Dominant Performance: Cryptocurrency revenue achieved a remarkable milestone, reaching $358 million and surpassing options trading as the primary revenue source. This represents a 700% year-over-year increase. The platform's total transaction-based revenue jumped to $672 million, up 200% from the prior year. Options revenue, previously the platform's main revenue generator, generated $222 million—still a solid 83% increase. Equities contributed $61 million, reflecting a 144% gain. Overall Financial Strength: Beyond transaction revenue, Robinhood generated $296 million from interest income (up 25%) and $46 million from subscriptions. The company's net income surged to $916 million, benefiting from a $369 million deferred tax benefit and a $55 million regulatory settlement reversal. Customer Growth: The platform's customer base expanded meaningfully with funded customers increasing 8% to 25.2 million and investment accounts rising 10% to 26.2 million. Average revenue per user doubled to $164, while net deposits reached $16.1 billion. Robinhood stock surged 15.3% in after-hours trading, reaching an all-time high of $64.47 per share.
